displayTitle: Sharp or Fuzzy: Reading MT Indications

beats.narration: Two questions decide whether magnetic particle testing shows you a discontinuity: what's coating the surface, and how deep it sits.

payload.title: SHARP OR FUZZY?
payload.sub: How coatings and depth decide what you see.

beats.narration: Leakage flux fades fast above the surface. A nonmagnetic coating holds particles up in the weak zone: thin films cost little, thick coatings erase indications.

payload.heading: How a coating steals sensitivity
payload.note: THIN FILM COSTS LITTLE · THICK ERASES

beats.narration: A surface crack breaks the flux abruptly: sharp, dense, tightly held. A buried flaw leaks only a diluted field: broad, fuzzy, easily wiped.
Your current decides. A C rides the skin: surface only. Half-wave pulses and penetrates. Full-wave D C reaches deepest, about a quarter inch at best.
Read the edges. Sharp, dense, tightly held: surface until proven otherwise. Broad and fuzzy: it's buried. Could your current even reach it?

payload.sub: Could your current even reach it?
